eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.method_note: RAM is considered included in compute cost and is not monetized separately in the operationalized framework model. The adopted MCC assumes a non-pruned full node with full blockchain replication for structural comparability with other replicated-ledger technologies.
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Bitcoin Core full node requirements; framework normalized cost model

ps_throughput.source: Zhang et al., Speeding up block propagation in Bitcoin network, 2022

eco_variable_transaction_cost.conversion_note: 0.3466 USD/tx converted using ECB reference rate 1 EUR = 1.1476 USD
eco_variable_transaction_cost.source: YCharts Bitcoin Average Transaction Fee, 15 March 2026; ECB USD reference exchange rate, 13 March 2026

eco_storage_footprint.source: Bitcoin Core full node requirements; framework comparative baseline assumption

parallel_execution.motivation: No native real parallel ledger execution.

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: No native sharding or horizontal partitioning.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: Global serialization on a single blockchain is mandatory.

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: Coordination remains globally serialized via PoW and longest-chain logic.

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: Each block includes the hash of the previous block.

cryptographic_write_authentication.motivation: Transactions are cryptographically authenticated by signatures and scripts.

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: Full nodes can independently verify ledger validity and integrity.

rci_tamper_evidence_auditability.source: Bitcoin Developer Guide; Bitcoin block reference; Bitcoin transactions reference; Bitcoin full node docs

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: Chain reorganization is possible, so commit is not structurally irreversible.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: Confirmation depends on chain extension by multiple actors.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: A single ordinary actor cannot unilaterally roll back valid state.

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: The Bitcoin model does not depend on a single structurally privileged writer.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: The model is not quorum-based in the classical BFT or replica-set sense.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: Write continuation is automatic as long as active hashpower exists.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: Failure of a single node does not structurally block write capability.

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.source: Bitcoin Developer Guide; Bitcoin full node docs

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: No single entity has unilateral structural authority over valid state.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: Validation is structurally distributed across independent actors.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: Participation does not require discretionary admission by a central authority.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: The validator set is not structurally closed.

ags_control_distribution_governance.source: Bitcoin full node docs; Bitcoin Developer Guide

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: The protocol does not provide native organizational role differentiation.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: There is no native identity lifecycle governance.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: There is no native distributed identity governance layer.

ags_identity_access_governance.source: Bitcoin transactions reference; Bitcoin Developer Guide

non_unilateral_compromise_possible.motivation: A single ordinary actor cannot unilaterally compromise the ledger.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: A reliable attack on transaction history requires majority hashpower.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: The relevant threshold is majority, not supermajority.
